data.title: Antares Receives DOE Documented Safety Analysis Approval for Mark-0 Demonstration Reactor
data.description: Antares, an advanced nuclear energy company based in Idaho Falls, has recently obtained approval from the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) for the Documented Safety Analysis of its Mark-0 reactor. This approval, in accordance with DOE standard 1271, comes after Antares previously received approval for its Preliminary Documented Safety Analysis in January 2026. This signifies a significant milestone as the DOE has accepted the final design of the Mark-0 reactor along with its safety case. Antares will now progress into the DOE Readiness Review stage, the concluding phase in this process, marking a crucial step forward for the company.

data.title: Bitcoin Is Going To $10,000, Says Bloomberg Analyst — But Others Call It 'Rage Bait'
data.description: Commodity analyst Mike McGlone from Bloomberg has made a bold prediction concerning Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC), forecasting a drop to $10,000 despite facing criticism from other analysts who dismissed the forecast as "rage bait." McGlone's basis for this prediction lies in the typical mean reversion patterns observed in commodities. Drawing parallels to crude oil's historical cycle from $120 to $40, he anticipates a similar trajectory for Bitcoin, attributing the drop to $10,000 as part of a larger market downturn where the S&P 500 (NYSE: SPY) could decline by 50-60%. He asserts that this projection is not a result of faulty calculations but rather a reflection of standard reversion trends. McGlone emphasizes the intense competition Bitcoin faces from other cryptocurrencies and its significantly higher volatility compared to the S&P 500, suggesting that it may not be a reliable store of value. Additionally, he anticipates crude oil hitting $50 by midterm elections and projects natural gas to remain at $2.84. For further details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Robinhood, BNY Win Trump Accounts Contract As Signups Reach 4 Million
data.description: Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ: HOOD) and BNY (NYSE: BK) have secured the contract to develop and manage tax-deferred investing accounts for children known as Trump Accounts. The U.S. Department of the Treasury announced that BNY will act as the financial agent to oversee the accounts, with over 4 million children already enrolled as of March 31. BNY collaborated with Robinhood to create the Trump Accounts app and offer customer support for the initiative, set to launch on July 4. Vlad Tenev, the Chairman and CEO of Robinhood, stated, "Our goal is to provide the next generation of Americans with a top-notch, user-friendly platform to kickstart their financial journey." The app will be customized with a white-label design specific to the Treasury, devoid of BNY or Robinhood branding. The financial details of the contract remain undisclosed. Various Wall Street firms have expressed interest in participating in the program, viewing the accounts as a valuable opportunity.
